mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and higher recession probability, causing equity markets to price in earnings downgrades over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real personal income and a deceleration in core inflation indicates a soft landing environment where corporate margins expand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a month-over-month increase in industrial production signals resilient supply-side capacity that offsets rate hike headwinds, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when the FEDFUNDS slope is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which translates directly into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.2 million units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and broader consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of instances when HOUST drops below 1200, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of stable federal funds rates and declining unemployment indicates a healthy economic expansion without aggressive tightening, supporting positive equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, accelerating real GDP growth drives corporate earnings revisions upward, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery regimes, or the predictive IC drops below 0.02 in out-of-sample tests.
